_comment: ISC Mathematics syllabus structure. Source: CISCE ISC 2025-26 spec. Used as the topic picker source — the LLM generates questions on the chosen topic.

chapters.label: Sets
chapters.description: Set notation, types of sets, subsets, power sets, universal set, Venn diagrams, union, intersection, complement, difference, and cardinality formulas including the inclusion-exclusion principle.

chapters.label: Relations and Functions
chapters.description: Ordered pairs, Cartesian product, relations, domain and range, types of relations, functions as special relations, one-one, onto, and composition of functions.

chapters.label: Trigonometric Functions
chapters.description: Angles and their measures (degrees and radians), trigonometric ratios, identities, graphs of sin/cos/tan, trigonometric equations, and general solutions.

chapters.label: Principle of Mathematical Induction
chapters.description: Statement of the principle, proving formulas for sums, divisibility, and inequalities using the base case and inductive step.

chapters.label: Complex Numbers and Quadratic Equations
chapters.description: Complex numbers as a+bi, algebra of complex numbers, modulus, conjugate, Argand plane, and solving quadratic equations with complex roots.

chapters.label: Linear Inequalities
chapters.description: Solving linear inequalities in one and two variables, graphical representation, and system of linear inequalities.

chapters.label: Permutations and Combinations
chapters.description: Fundamental counting principle, factorial notation, permutations (nPr), combinations (nCr), and applications to word problems.

chapters.label: Binomial Theorem
chapters.description: Binomial theorem for positive integral index, general term, middle term, and applications of binomial coefficients.

chapters.label: Sequences and Series
chapters.description: Arithmetic progressions (AP), geometric progressions (GP), sum of n terms, arithmetic and geometric means, and special series (sum of squares, cubes).

chapters.label: Straight Lines
chapters.description: Slope of a line, various forms of equations (slope-intercept, point-slope, two-point, intercept), angle between lines, distance from a point to a line, and family of lines.

chapters.label: Conic Sections
chapters.description: Circle, parabola, ellipse, and hyperbola — standard equations, focus, directrix, eccentricity, and latus rectum.

chapters.label: Introduction to Three-Dimensional Geometry
chapters.description: Coordinate axes in 3D, distance formula, section formula, and coordinates of a point dividing a line segment.

chapters.label: Limits and Derivatives
chapters.description: Intuitive idea of limits, limit laws, limits of polynomials and rational functions, definition of derivative, derivative of sum/difference/product/quotient, and derivatives of polynomial and trigonometric functions.

chapters.label: Mathematical Reasoning
chapters.description: Statements, logical connectives (and, or, not, implies, if and only if), quantifiers, validity of statements, and methods of proof.

chapters.label: Statistics
chapters.description: Measures of dispersion — range, mean deviation, variance, and standard deviation for grouped and ungrouped data.

chapters.label: Probability
chapters.description: Random experiments, sample spaces, events, classical and axiomatic probability, addition theorem, and complementary events.

chapters.label: Relations and Functions
chapters.description: Types of relations (reflexive, symmetric, transitive, equivalence), types of functions (injective, surjective, bijective), composition and invertible functions, and binary operations.

chapters.label: Inverse Trigonometric Functions
chapters.description: Definition, domain, range, and principal value branches of inverse trigonometric functions, and properties of inverse trig functions.

chapters.label: Matrices
chapters.description: Types of matrices, matrix operations (addition, scalar multiplication, multiplication), transpose, symmetric and skew-symmetric matrices, and elementary row/column operations.

chapters.label: Determinants
chapters.description: Determinant of a square matrix (up to 3x3), properties, cofactors, adjoint, inverse of a matrix, and solving systems of linear equations using Cramer's rule.

chapters.label: Continuity and Differentiability
chapters.description: Continuity of functions, differentiability, chain rule, derivatives of implicit functions, exponential and logarithmic functions, logarithmic differentiation, and Rolle's and Mean Value theorems.

chapters.label: Applications of Derivatives
chapters.description: Rate of change, increasing/decreasing functions, tangents and normals, maxima and minima (first and second derivative tests), and applied optimization problems.

chapters.label: Integrals
chapters.description: Indefinite integrals (substitution, partial fractions, by parts), definite integrals, fundamental theorem of calculus, and properties of definite integrals.

chapters.label: Applications of Integrals
chapters.description: Area under curves, area between two curves using integration.

chapters.label: Differential Equations
chapters.description: Order and degree, formation of differential equations, methods of solution (variable separable, homogeneous, linear first-order), and applications.

chapters.label: Vector Algebra
chapters.description: Vectors and scalars, magnitude and direction, position vectors, addition, scalar multiplication, dot product, cross product, and scalar triple product.

chapters.label: Three-Dimensional Geometry
chapters.description: Direction cosines and ratios, equations of a line in space, angle between lines, shortest distance, equation of a plane, angle between planes, and distance of a point from a plane.

chapters.label: Linear Programming
chapters.description: Formulation of LP problems, graphical method of solution, feasible and infeasible regions, optimal solution, and bounded/unbounded regions.

chapters.label: Probability
chapters.description: Conditional probability, multiplication theorem, independent events, Bayes' theorem, and probability distributions (mean and variance of random variables, binomial distribution).

computer-science.label: Computer Science (Phase 2 — coming soon)
computer-science._note: Roadmapped, not shipped in v1. Java code execution sandbox and SQL question handling are separate engineering problems.
